import DefaultLayout from "/home/runner/work/metafox-dev-docs/metafox-dev-docs/node_modules/gatsby-theme-docz/src/base/Layout.js";
export const _frontmatter = {};
const layoutProps = {
  _frontmatter
};
const MDXLayout = DefaultLayout;
export default function MDXContent({
  components,
  ...props
}) {
  return <MDXLayout {...layoutProps} {...props} components={components} mdxType="MDXLayout">


    <h1 {...{
      "id": "service-manager"
    }}>{`Service Manager`}</h1>
    <p>{`Service can be a class instance, a function or a React component to handle some pieces of your app logic.`}</p>
    <p>{`In MetaFox, platform has a Service Manager called `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`globalContext`}</inlineCode>{` to manage all services. You can not access the global `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manager`}</inlineCode>{` directly but via hooks or saga context instead.`}</p>
    <p>{`A service after passed to global service manager can be accessible in React components or Saga functions.`}</p>
    <p>{`In React component:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-tsx"
      }}>{`import {useGlobal} from '@metafox/framework'

const MyComponent (){
  const manager = useGlobal();
  const {apiClient} =  manager

  // or simplier
  // const {apiClient} =  useGlobal();
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`In saga functions:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-ts"
      }}>{`import { getContext } from 'redux-saga/effects';

export function mySagas() {
  const { apiClient } = yield * getGlobalContext();
}
`}</code></pre>
    <blockquote>
      <p parentName="blockquote"><inlineCode parentName="p">{`globalContext`}</inlineCode>{` is mutable, changeble. Its service members will be affected by others without being changed or updated.`}</p>
    </blockquote>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "class-services"
    }}>{`Class Services`}</h2>
    <p>{`A standard Service class must have `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`bootstrap`}</inlineCode>{` method, DO NOT define `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`bootstrap`}</inlineCode>{` as an arrow function.`}</p>
    <p>{`Let's create the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`LogService`}</inlineCode>{` by adding the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`src/manager/LogService.ts`}</inlineCode>{` with the following content`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-tsx"
      }}>{`/**
 * @type: service
 * name: logService
 **/

import {Manager} from '@metafox/manager'

type LogServiceProps {
  // any property here
}

class LogService {
  options: LogServiceProps;
  constructor(config: LogServiceProps){
    this.config = LogServiceProps
  }

  bootstrap(manager: Manager){
    // to do something here.
    // return this or void
  }
  info(message: string){
    console.log(message)
  }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Restart dev server, then you can use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`LogService`}</inlineCode>{` with `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`useGlobal`}</inlineCode>{` hook as the below example:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-tsx"
      }}>{`import useGlobal from '@metafox/framework';

const LoginForm (){
  const { logService } = useGlobal();
  logService.info('This message is passed by logService')
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "configuration"
    }}>{`Configuration`}</h2>
    <p>{`In the above example, you have created a service named `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`logService`}</inlineCode>{`. You can pass configuration from root app by adding a section with the same name in `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` as below`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  "logService": {
    "level": "warn"
  }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Service Manager will pass this configuration to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`LogService`}</inlineCode>{` constructor.`}</p>
    <p>{`If you want to access global configuration in `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`LogService`}</inlineCode>{`, just use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manager.options`}</inlineCode>{` accesstor.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "functional-service"
    }}>{`Functional Service`}</h2>
    <p>{`Sometimes, you would like to declare general functions which can be used anywhere in React components and Sagas. And, you don't want to declare `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`import`}</inlineCode>{` dependencies everywhere. The good solution is to inject them into Service Manager.`}</p>
    <p>{`In the next example, we will define a function to generate random string`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-tsx"
      }}>{`export default function randomId(): string {
  return Math.random()
    .toString(36)
    .replace(/[^a-z]+/g, '')
    .substr(0, 5);
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Now, pass the function to Service Manager`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-tsx"
      }}>{`# file src/manager.tsx

const manager = {
  logService: LogService,
  randomId,
  // other manager here
}

export default manager
`}</code></pre>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-tsx"
      }}>{`import {useGlobal} from '@metafox/framework';

const LoginForm (){
  const { logService, randomId } = useGlobal();
  logService.info('This message is passed by logService')
  logService.info('test message id:', randomId());
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "react-component-service"
    }}>{`React Component Service`}</h2>
    <p>{`In order to inject a React Component across apps without declaration for dependencies, the best way is to inject components into the global Service Manager by using `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`inject`}</inlineCode>{` method`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-tsx"
      }}>{`/**
 * @type: service
 * name: CommentList
 */

export default function CommentList() {}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Next, we will declare typings`}</p>
    <p>{`Create `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`./src/module.d.ts`}</inlineCode>{` with the following content`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-tsx"
      }}>{`import '@metafox/framework/Manager';
declare module '@metafox/framework/Manager' {
  interface Manager {
    CommentList?: React.FC<{}>;
  }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Now, you can get CommentList from other component. For example:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-tsx"
      }}>{`import { useGlobal } from '@metafox/framework';

function MyComponent() {
  const { CommentList } = useGlobal();

  return <CommentList />;
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "core-services"
    }}>{`Core Services`}</h2>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`apiClient`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`usePopover`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`cookieBackend`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`normalization`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`preferenceBackend`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`dialogBackend`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`intl`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`createPageParams`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`localStore`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`compactUrl`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`slugify`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`copyToClipboard`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`useActionControl`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`useSession`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`useGetItem`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`useLoggedIn`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`useIsMobile`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`usePageParams`}</li>
    </ul>

    </MDXLayout>;
}
;
MDXContent.isMDXComponent = true;
